narrative:
On June 11, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 40.2%, resolving 1,141 cases out of 2,839 scheduled. This performance represents a continued downward trend in clearance efficiency over the preceding three days, falling from 47.5% on June 9 and 42.8% on June 10, though remaining close to the 41.0% conversion rate observed on June 8. 

Individual judge outputs varied significantly. Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty in Court Hall 19 managed the day's heaviest load, listing 507 cases but converting only 15.8% (80 cases) into active hearings. Conversely, Justice V. Srishananda in Court Hall 21 achieved a high efficiency rate, hearing 70 of 84 scheduled matters (83.3%). Justice M. Nagaprasanna in Court Hall 14 oversaw multiple lists, notably hearing 58 out of 75 cases (77.3%) in one session, while converting 23.8% of a larger 151-case block in another.

In terms of procedural stages, Orders comprised the largest category with 989 scheduled and 460 heard, mimicking the exact 46.5% conversion rate seen on June 10. Final Hearings lagged behind, converting only 20.2% of 550 scheduled matters. Writ Petitions (WP) dominated the case type distribution with 1,174 listings but yielded a modest 33.0% hearing rate. Conversely, Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) saw a stronger conversion of 51.0% (156 of 306), and Regular Second Appeals (RSA) achieved a high clearance conversion of 70.8% (85 of 120).
